# `xterm-modes` sequences

<!-- Generated by `qdb generate reference` from `db/xterm-modes.toml`. Do not edit by hand. -->

## DECSET Application Cursor Keys (`dec.mode.application_cursor_keys.set`)

Changes cursor-key encoding between normal and application mode.

- Direction: host-to-terminal
- Syntax: `CSI ? 1 h`
- References: [XTerm Control Sequences (invisible-island)](https://www.invisible-island.net/xterm/ctlseqs/ctlseqs.html) (retrieved 2026-07-06)
- Fixtures: [`fixtures/xterm/dec_mode_1_set.seq`](../../../fixtures/xterm/dec_mode_1_set.seq)

## DECRST Application Cursor Keys (`dec.mode.application_cursor_keys.reset`)

Returns the cursor keys to normal (ANSI) mode so they send standard sequences.

- Direction: host-to-terminal
- Syntax: `CSI ? 1 l`
- References: [XTerm Control Sequences (invisible-island)](https://www.invisible-island.net/xterm/ctlseqs/ctlseqs.html) (retrieved 2026-07-06)
- Fixtures: [`fixtures/xterm/dec_mode_1_reset.seq`](../../../fixtures/xterm/dec_mode_1_reset.seq)

## DECRQM Application Cursor Keys (`dec.mode.application_cursor_keys.query`)

Fixture exercises generic DECRQM; response routing is keyed by mode number.

- Direction: host-to-terminal
- Syntax: `CSI ? 1 $ p`
- Responds with: `dec.mode.application_cursor_keys.report`
- References: [XTerm Control Sequences (invisible-island)](https://www.invisible-island.net/xterm/ctlseqs/ctlseqs.html) (retrieved 2026-07-06)
- Fixtures: [`fixtures/xterm/dec_mode_1_query.seq`](../../../fixtures/xterm/dec_mode_1_query.seq)

## DECRPM Application Cursor Keys (`dec.mode.application_cursor_keys.report`)

Fixture reports reset state.

- Direction: terminal-to-host
- References: [XTerm Control Sequences (invisible-island)](https://www.invisible-island.net/xterm/ctlseqs/ctlseqs.html) (retrieved 2026-07-06)
- Notes: reply syntax pending live capture

## DECPAM Application Keypad (`dec.application_keypad.set`)

Switches the numeric keypad to VT application-keypad escape reports.

- Direction: host-to-terminal
- Syntax: `ESC =`
- References: [XTerm Control Sequences (invisible-island)](https://www.invisible-island.net/xterm/ctlseqs/ctlseqs.html) (retrieved 2026-07-06)
- Fixtures: [`fixtures/xterm/application_keypad_set.seq`](../../../fixtures/xterm/application_keypad_set.seq)

## DECPNM Numeric Keypad (`dec.application_keypad.reset`)

Restores the numeric keypad to normal digit/operator reporting.

- Direction: host-to-terminal
- Syntax: `ESC >`
- References: [XTerm Control Sequences (invisible-island)](https://www.invisible-island.net/xterm/ctlseqs/ctlseqs.html) (retrieved 2026-07-06)
- Fixtures: [`fixtures/xterm/application_keypad_reset.seq`](../../../fixtures/xterm/application_keypad_reset.seq)

## DECSET Origin Mode (`dec.mode.origin.set`)

Makes cursor positions relative to scrolling margins.

- Direction: host-to-terminal
- Syntax: `CSI ? 6 h`
- References: [XTerm Control Sequences (invisible-island)](https://www.invisible-island.net/xterm/ctlseqs/ctlseqs.html) (retrieved 2026-07-06)
- Fixtures: [`fixtures/xterm/dec_mode_6_set.seq`](../../../fixtures/xterm/dec_mode_6_set.seq)

## DECRST Origin Mode (`dec.mode.origin.reset`)

Disables origin mode so cursor coordinates are relative to the whole screen.

- Direction: host-to-terminal
- Syntax: `CSI ? 6 l`
- References: [XTerm Control Sequences (invisible-island)](https://www.invisible-island.net/xterm/ctlseqs/ctlseqs.html) (retrieved 2026-07-06)
- Fixtures: [`fixtures/xterm/dec_mode_6_reset.seq`](../../../fixtures/xterm/dec_mode_6_reset.seq)

## DECRQM Origin Mode (`dec.mode.origin.query`)

Queries whether cursor coordinates are absolute or relative to scroll margins.

- Direction: host-to-terminal
- Syntax: `CSI ? 6 $ p`
- Responds with: `dec.mode.origin.report`
- References: [XFree86 Control Sequences](https://www.xfree86.org/current/ctlseqs.html) (retrieved 2026-07-06)
- Fixtures: [`fixtures/xterm/dec_mode_6_query.seq`](../../../fixtures/xterm/dec_mode_6_query.seq)

## DECRPM Origin Mode (`dec.mode.origin.report`)

Fixture reports reset state.

- Direction: terminal-to-host
- References: [XFree86 Control Sequences](https://www.xfree86.org/current/ctlseqs.html) (retrieved 2026-07-06)
- Notes: reply syntax pending live capture

## DECSET Autowrap Mode (`dec.mode.autowrap.set`)

Controls whether printing past the right margin wraps to the next line.

- Direction: host-to-terminal
- Syntax: `CSI ? 7 h`
- References: [XTerm Control Sequences (invisible-island)](https://www.invisible-island.net/xterm/ctlseqs/ctlseqs.html) (retrieved 2026-07-06)
- Fixtures: [`fixtures/xterm/dec_mode_7_set.seq`](../../../fixtures/xterm/dec_mode_7_set.seq)

## DECRST Autowrap Mode (`dec.mode.autowrap.reset`)

Disables autowrap so text at the right margin overwrites the last column.

- Direction: host-to-terminal
- Syntax: `CSI ? 7 l`
- References: [XTerm Control Sequences (invisible-island)](https://www.invisible-island.net/xterm/ctlseqs/ctlseqs.html) (retrieved 2026-07-06)
- Fixtures: [`fixtures/xterm/dec_mode_7_reset.seq`](../../../fixtures/xterm/dec_mode_7_reset.seq)

## DECRQM Autowrap Mode (`dec.mode.autowrap.query`)

Queries DECAWM without changing wrap behavior.

- Direction: host-to-terminal
- Syntax: `CSI ? 7 $ p`
- Responds with: `dec.mode.autowrap.report`
- References: [XFree86 Control Sequences](https://www.xfree86.org/current/ctlseqs.html) (retrieved 2026-07-06)
- Fixtures: [`fixtures/xterm/dec_mode_7_query.seq`](../../../fixtures/xterm/dec_mode_7_query.seq)

## DECRPM Autowrap Mode (`dec.mode.autowrap.report`)

Fixture reports reset state.

- Direction: terminal-to-host
- References: [XFree86 Control Sequences](https://www.xfree86.org/current/ctlseqs.html) (retrieved 2026-07-06)
- Notes: reply syntax pending live capture

## DECSET Cursor Visible (`dec.mode.cursor_visible.set`)

Equivalent to the xterm cursor-show helper.

- Direction: host-to-terminal
- Syntax: `CSI ? 25 h`
- References: [XTerm Control Sequences (invisible-island)](https://www.invisible-island.net/xterm/ctlseqs/ctlseqs.html) (retrieved 2026-07-06)
- Fixtures: [`fixtures/xterm/dec_mode_25_set.seq`](../../../fixtures/xterm/dec_mode_25_set.seq)

## DECRQM Cursor Visible (`dec.mode.cursor_visible.query`)

Queries DECTCEM cursor visibility.

- Direction: host-to-terminal
- Syntax: `CSI ? 25 $ p`
- Responds with: `dec.mode.cursor_visible.report`
- References: [XFree86 Control Sequences](https://www.xfree86.org/current/ctlseqs.html) (retrieved 2026-07-06)
- Fixtures: [`fixtures/xterm/dec_mode_25_query.seq`](../../../fixtures/xterm/dec_mode_25_query.seq)

## DECRPM Cursor Visible (`dec.mode.cursor_visible.report`)

Fixture reports set state, matching a visible cursor.

- Direction: terminal-to-host
- References: [XFree86 Control Sequences](https://www.xfree86.org/current/ctlseqs.html) (retrieved 2026-07-06)
- Notes: reply syntax pending live capture

## DECRST Cursor Visible (`dec.mode.cursor_visible.reset`)

Equivalent to the xterm cursor-hide helper.

- Direction: host-to-terminal
- Syntax: `CSI ? 25 l`
- References: [XTerm Control Sequences (invisible-island)](https://www.invisible-island.net/xterm/ctlseqs/ctlseqs.html) (retrieved 2026-07-06)
- Fixtures: [`fixtures/xterm/dec_mode_25_reset.seq`](../../../fixtures/xterm/dec_mode_25_reset.seq)

## DECRQM Synchronized Output (2026) (`dec.mode.synchronized_output.query`)

Probes mode 2026; the DA1-fenced capability bundle routes the reply by mode number.

- Direction: host-to-terminal
- Syntax: `CSI ? 2026 $ p`
- Responds with: `dec.mode.synchronized_output.report`
- References: [Contour: Synchronized Output VT extension](https://contour-terminal.org/vt-extensions/synchronized-output/) (retrieved 2026-07-06)
- Conformance: alacritty unprobeable; betamax unprobeable; foot unprobeable; kitty unprobeable; tmux unprobeable; wezterm unprobeable; xterm unprobeable

## DECRPM Synchronized Output (2026) (`dec.mode.synchronized_output.report`)

Reports whether mode 2026 (synchronized output) is set, reset, or not recognized.

- Direction: terminal-to-host
- References: [Contour: Synchronized Output VT extension](https://contour-terminal.org/vt-extensions/synchronized-output/) (retrieved 2026-07-06)
- Notes: reply syntax pending live capture; the shared DECRPM event preserves mode and state.
